about.aDelegationToOurStakePool: A delegation to our stake pool
about.aboutPooltoolParagraph: PoolTool is an online platform built to facilitate the interaction between SPOs and their stakeholders. Our goal is to provide our users with useful tools and the most up-to-date information on Cardano, including staking, in-depth research and exclusive offers from SPOs
about.ifYouHaveFeedback: If you have feedback or want to see a new feature you can open an issue on our github repo
about.ifYouLikeWhatWeDo: If you like what we do here then please offer up some gratitude in the form of either
about.itHasBeenALabor: It has been a labor of love for the community and I am happy to have had a small part in helping get the decentralization of Cardano going
about.noneOfThisCouldReallyBePossible: None of this could really be possible without the amazing feedback, support, coaching and answers I have received from countless people in the community
about.poolToolWasDevelopedOutOfMyDesire: PoolTool was developed out of my desire to see the blocks I was making on the various testnets realtime and how they compared to other pool operators

addressDetail.disclaimer: Please note that due to how we calculate rewards you may see some minor differences between daedalus and pooltool.io.  Please verify amounts match your wallet to your satisfaction.  PoolTool makes no warranty for this information.

app.alreadyVerified: This address has already been registered and verified by a different account. You are unable to claim it.
app.alreadyVerifiedLogin: This address has already been registered and verified. Please login.
app.ifClaimingPool: If you are trying to claim your pool then you will need to claim the REWARD address for your pool.
app.ifNoAccount: If you do not already have an account please
app.ifYouAlreadyClaimed: If you already have claimed a different address in PoolTool then you MUST be logged in before claming this one. If you do not log in first then you will forever have two separate accounts. If you log in first then we can combine your addresses together. Trust us. You want just one account.

networkHealth.asPartOfOurServiceToTheCommunity: As part of our service to the community we provide an endpoint where pools can voluntarily submit their latest block heights to pooltool

poolManage.extremelyLargeTopology: Extremely large topology with all nodes in the testnet can cause excessive load on your nodes
poolManage.givenAWellDistributedNetwork: Given a well distributed network, a topology of around 20 nodes should provide at most 3 hops to all nodes on the network
poolManage.givenThatNoCentralizedSystem: Given that no centralized system should every be relied on, there are other topology services also emerging from the cardano community
poolManage.ifItsAlreadyClaimed: If its already claimed by someone else (likely by mistake) then contact @papacarp on telegram
poolManage.ifYourFriendsFindYou: If your friends find you first their record will also show up here for you to approve
poolManage.makeSureYouIncludeTheIOHK: Make sure you include the IOHK nodes and your pool nodes
poolManage.pleaseConfirmThisIsWhatYouWantToDo: Please confirm this is what you want to do
poolManage.pleaseInvestigateTheseAsWell: Please investigate these as well and use whichever strategy (or all of them) that makes sense for your platform
poolManage.postANoteToYourPage: Post a note to your page or make an announcement to your
poolManage.putYourLocalPoolHere: Put your local pool here plus any fixed nodes you want to connect to (IOHK)
poolManage.releasingThisPoolWillRemoveAll: Releasing this pool will remove all configured information related to it from pooltool
poolManage.theConnectionIsNotEstablished: The connection is not established until there is a request and an approval
poolManage.theseAreYouPrivateNodes: These are you private nodes that allows pooltool to create your entire topology
poolManage.thisIsWhereYouSpecifyYourCustomGroup: This is where you specify your custom group of 20 nodes you want to connect to in your topology
poolManage.thisPoolWillRockYourWorld: This pool will rock your world
poolManage.toConfigureThisPool: To configure this pool please claim the pool first
poolManage.toDownloadYourTopologyUseThe: To download your topology use the
poolManage.untilP2PIsAvaliable: Until P2P is avaliable for the shelley cardano node we will need effective topology in place to keep you connected to surrounding nodes
poolManage.useThePlatformBelowToConnect: Use the platform below to connect with a group of 20 nodes on the network for each public facing relay you have
poolManage.useThisToPutANote: Use this to put a note on your pool that visitors will see on your pool page
poolManage.useThisToSendAMessage: Use this to send a message to all subscribers to your pool on the @pooltoolbot
poolManage.youCanEitherHavePooltool: You can either have pooltool host your metadata or download the json file to store on your server
poolManage.youMayUseItForAnything: You may use it for anything you want to say
poolManage.youProvideTheURLPlusAHash: You provide the URL plus a hash of the metadata at that location
poolManage.yourNodeDetailsAreNeverDisclosed: Your node details are never disclosed outside of the buddies you trust
poolManage.yourPoolMetadataUrl: Your pool metadata url is stored on chain when you register you pool

pools.k500satpoint1: Saturation level for stake pools will drop to
pools.k500satpoint2: on December 6, 2020 (as a result of k parameter adjustment from 150 to 500 by IOG). PoolTool suggests that you find a pool below
pools.k500satpoint3: prior to December 1 (one epoch ahead). Check the Saturation Curve tab on your favorite pool to see where they stand.
pools.lifeROSNotSortable: Lifetime ROS is displayed but not sorted on pools with less than 6 epochs of history
pools.poolToolBotIsBrought: PoolToolBot is brought to you by Erik the Red Stakepool
pools.saturatedk500: This pool will be saturated starting December 6th.  Move your stake from this pool to another unsaturated pool before December 1st.
pools.weWillKeepYou: We will keep you informed about any major events like blocks, delegation changes or fee changes on pools you follow

recentblocks.propDescription: Median propagation delay for the block
recentblocks.protoDescription: Protocol for the Block Producer.  Can be used to generally identify which version of software the block producer is using.
recentblocks.reportsDescription: Number of nodes that have reported this block

app.reloadPooltoolPrompt: We have upgraded PoolTool.io app. Please reload now.
app.resetPasswordDetails: To reset your password use any of your addresses that are already validated with pooltool. You must have the ability to send a small transaction from this wallet to verify your ownership of the address.
app.toAddress: to the following address FROM THE WALLET YOU ARE TRYING TO VERIFY (it doesn't have to be from the exact address you are verifying but needs to be from the same wallet associated with the address you are verifying)
app.toClaimYouMust: To claim this address you must be able to send a small amount of ADA from it to verify ownership. If this is a reward address for a pool then this will also enable you to edit your pool parameters for the pool.
app.toCreateAnAccount: To create an account please use a STAKED address from your wallet that you want to claim. It can either be a stake address (shorter) or a full length address (longer). In addition you must have the ability to send a small transaction from this wallet.
app.toVerify: To verify your ownership of this address please send exactly
app.toVerify1Hr: You have 1 hour to make this transaction to validate your account.

trackRewards.youCanUseOurForm: You can use our form below to paste your staking address, or try to find it in the delegator section of the pool you are currently delegated to

app.usernameExistsException: That email address has already been registered
app.warningNewLogin: This is a COMPLETELY new stake address based login system that replaces our old logins that required your email address.  Your old logins for PoolTool will no longer work.  If you have not created an account yet in the new system then you will need to do that first.

help.blockHeightAlignmentHelp: What is the Historical Block Height Alignment Chart?
help.blockHeightAlignmentHelp1: Often called the "Raindrops Chart" this chart shows us how all reporting nodes are staying up to date with the latest blocks. In an ideal world all nodes would report the same blocks at the same time, but in reality nodes often do not report the latest block all together for various reasons.
help.blockHeightAlignmentHelp2: Whatever block hieght the majority of nodes are on is considered the reference point, and is where 0 is on the left side of the chart. Any node that has stopped reporting will slowly get left behind as the majority of nodes carry on.  At first a node that has stopped reporting will be at -1 on the left side axis, then -2, then -3 and so on.  Nodes that stop reporting look like raindrops in this chart as they fall away from the pack.
help.blockHeightAlignmentHelp3: nodes are stopped/started all the time due to routine maintenance which will cause these raindrops.  In addition, sometimes nodes will get stuck due to misconfiguration, network issue, etc.  Those also will look like these raindrops.
help.blockHeightAlignmentHelp4: We use this chart to get an idea of node syncronization, and keep an eye out for large scale failure modes.
